title: Bhoga Nandeeshwara Temple
tagline: Bhoga Nandeeshwara Temple — spiritual town of Karnataka

overview.short: Bhoganandiswara Temple and Arunachaleswara Temple are a twin Hindu temples complex located in Nandi village in Chikkaballapur district of Karnataka, India.
overview.description: Bhoganandiswara Temple and Arunachaleswara Temple are a twin Hindu temples complex located in Nandi village in Chikkaballapur district of Karnataka, India. Ornate, beautifully carved and dedicated to Shiva, they have been variously dated between the 9th- to 10th-century CE. The Bhoganandiswara is the northern temple of the twin. It is the oldest surviving temple in Nolambavadi-style of Dravidian architecture in Karnataka. The Arunachaleswara temple was added to its south shortly thereafter.

topPlaces.name: Siege of Nundydroog
topPlaces.description: The Siege of Nundydroog was conducted by British Indian forces under the command of General Charles Cornwallis in October 1791, during the Third Anglo-Mysore War. The fortress of Nundydroog, was held by the forces of Tipu Sultan, the ruler of Mysore.

topPlaces.name: Nandi Hills, India
topPlaces.description: Nandi Hills (Anglicised forms include Nandidurg and Nandydoorg) is an ancient hill fortress built by the Ganga dynasty in the Chikkaballapur district of Karnataka state. It is now a locality situated 10 km from Chickballapur town and approximately 60 km from Bengaluru.

topPlaces.name: Muddenahalli
topPlaces.description: Muddenahalli is a village situated 7 km from Chikballapur in Chikballapur District of Karnataka State. It is the birthplace of M. Visvesvaraya, one of India's most accomplished engineers.

topPlaces.name: Yoga Nandeeshwara Temple
topPlaces.description: Yoga Nandeeshwara Temple is a Hindu temple in Nandi Hills or Nandidurg, Chikkaballapur district, Karnataka, India, is a dedicated to the Lord Shiva. It dates back to the Chola's Period.

topPlaces.name: Skandagiri
topPlaces.description: Skandagiri, also known as Kalavara Durga, is a mountain fortress located approximately 62 km (39 mi) from Bangalore city, and 3 km (1.9 mi) from Chikballapur in the Indian state of Karnataka. It is off Bellary Road (National Highway 7 Hyderabad-Bangalore Highway), and overlooks Nandi Hills and Muddenahalli.

topPlaces.name: Chikkaballapur
topPlaces.description: Chikkaballapur or Chikkaballāpura is the district headquarters of the newly created Chikkaballapur district in the state of Karnataka, India, which was carved out from Kolar district. It is located within 3km of Muddenahalli (the birthplace of eminent engineer and statesman Sir Mokshagundam Visvesvarayya).

topPlaces.name: Agalagurki
topPlaces.description: Agalagurki is a village in the southern state of Karnataka, India. It is located in the Chikkaballapur taluk of Chikkaballapura district in Karnataka.

topPlaces.name: Mustur
topPlaces.description: Mustur is a village in the southern state of Karnataka, India. It is located in the Gangawati taluk of Koppal district in Karnataka.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Bhoga Nandeeshwara Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Bhoga Nandeeshwara Temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Bhoga Nandeeshwara Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Kempegowda International Airport, Bengaluru (21 km) and the nearest railway station is KSR Bengaluru City Junction (48 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Bhoga Nandeeshwara Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Bhoga Nandeeshwara Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Bhoga Nandeeshwara Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Bhoga Nandeeshwara Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Bhoga Nandeeshwara Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Siege of Nundydroog, Nandi Hills, India, Muddenahalli, Yoga Nandeeshwara Temple, Skandagiri.
